matches.rationale: The Bash rule is triggered when creating a pull request and its Korean evidence explicitly requires running make format in the modified library directory beforehand. It also combines make lint and make test, but under the 1:1 rule it is assigned only to the first and equally close gold rule, g1.
The Edit rule blocks the same Sphinx-style double-backtick inline-code form, and its evidence explicitly scopes the prohibition to docstrings and comments.

misses.note: The compiler merged make lint with the format and test requirements into pr-run-checks-before-create. That extracted rule is already assigned to the equally close g1 and cannot be reused under the 1:1 constraint.

The compiler merged make test with the format and lint requirements into pr-run-checks-before-create. That extracted rule is already assigned to the equally close g1 and cannot be reused under the 1:1 constraint.
Compiler-reported skip: "TEST 변수로 특정 테스트 파일·pytest 옵션 지정 가능하다는 설명은 사용 방법 안내(문서화)일 뿐 의무·금지·순서 지시가 없어 규칙 대상 아님"

The explicit positive clause requiring single backticks appears in the same compound bullet as the double-backtick prohibition, but the compiler extracted only the prohibition and produced no separate Edit rule for using single backticks.

unmatched_extracted.note: This is a real action-bindable Write prohibition supported by the document's instruction against double backticks in docstrings and comments, but the frozen gold ruleset lists only the corresponding Edit rule.
